title: Corydalis ecalcarata
taxonomy: Corydalis ecalcarata is classified within the family Papaveraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as poppies and their allies. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. The genus Corydalis itself is a part of the tribe Corydaleae. Corydalis ecalcarata is further classified as a species within the genus Corydalis. The exact classification may be subject to change based on ongoing research and taxonomic revisions.
uses: Corydalis ecalcarata, a lesser-known species of the Corydalis genus, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. However, the exact scope and efficacy of its medicinal uses are not well-documented in scientific literature. Some studies suggest that Corydalis species may possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. The plant has also been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Corydalis ecalcarata for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Corydalis ecalcarata contains a variety of active constituents, including alkaloids, which are a major class of compounds found in this plant species. Specifically, corydaline and protopine alkaloids are some of the key active constituents in Corydalis ecalcarata. These alkaloids are known for their potential medicinal properties, including analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's active constituents may also possess other bioactive properties, such as antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. intro: Corydalis ecalcarata, also known as Himalayan Corydalis or Yarsagumba, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues, due to its sedative and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Corydalis ecalcarata include alkaloids, such as yarsagumba alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional Tibetan and Nepalese medicine, Corydalis ecalcarata is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or capsule to harness its health benefits.
